docs.title: Dworshak CLI
docs.text: The Dworshak CLI orchestrates credential, configuration, and environment management.

docs.title: Command Tree
docs.text:
Core command groups:

Command Purpose secret encrypted credential storage config JSON configuration management env .env style variables prompt user input interface

See individual command pages for full help output.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

The format is (read: strives to be) based on Keep a Changelog.

docs.title: Changed:
docs.text: • Update helptree reference in docs/index.md.

docs.title: Changed:
docs.text:
• dworshak-prompt to 0.2.26
  • dworshak-secret to 1.2.15
  • Vault initialization is now automated if missing.

docs.title: Fixed:
docs.text: • Correct usage of --emit flag in bash scripting examples in README.md.

docs.title: Changed:
docs.text: • Update helptree and readme reference.

docs.title: Changed:
docs.text:
• Increase deps to:
    • dworshak-config==0.2.5
    • dworshak-env==0.1.6
    • dworshak-secret==1.2.14
    • dworshak-prompt==0.2.25
  • Campaign for stderr and stdout clarity, to benefit variable assignment and piping in the console.

docs.title: Changed:
docs.text:
• dworshak-prompt to 0.2.24
  • dworshak-secret to 1.2.11
  • Change build_pyz.yml instances of manage-vault to vault.
  • Update helptree and its reference in README.

docs.title: Changed:
docs.text:
• Set dworshak-env dep to 0.1.5
  • Set dworshak-prompt to 0.2.23
  • Set dworshak-config to 0.2.4
  • Update helptree svg

docs.title: Changed:
docs.text:
• Set dworshak-secret dep to 1.2.10
  • Set dworshak-prompt to 0.2.22
  • Set dworshak-config to 0.2.3
  • dworshak-config now has list and remove sub commands

docs.title: Internal:
docs.text:
• Known inconsistencies:
    • dworshak-env does not have list and remove commands
    • dworshak-prompt have --verbose and --debug commands but the other sub apps do not.
    • dworshak-config has --path flags but inconsistently have -p as an abbreviated version.
  • We need to collapse down a lot of the redudancy in dworshak-prompt. Are kwargs our friend?

docs.title: Changed
docs.text:
• Improve README.
  • Stop including basic --help screenshot. It is a maintenance risk/burden.

docs.title: Changed
docs.text:
• Set typer-helptree dep to 1.2.6
  • Set dworshak-secret dep to 1.2.8
  • Set dworshak-prompt to 0.2.20

docs.title: Added:
docs.text: • Leveraged new helptree --export-svg --assets which is great (!), to handle screenshots saved to ./assets/. This is way better.

docs.title: Added:
docs.text: • Dummy 'typer' extra, for ecosystem consistency; i have several time tried edit other lines line 'uv add dwroshak-prompt --extra typer --extra crypto', to apply the same line for dworshak, to save typing, and then get punished with a 'typer extra does not exist'. So now it exists.

docs.title: Changed:
docs.text: • Update ecosystem dependencies for CLI standardization and bug squashing.

docs.title: Changed:
docs.text: • Import dworshak-secret cli into cli.py. Remove redundant dead code.

docs.title: Fixed:
docs.text: • typer-helptree to 0.2.5, to correct sub apps interleaved with commands, alphabetizing but not giving thw obvious recursive levels.

docs.title: Internal:
docs.text: • Screenshot asset is now firmly out of date.

docs.title: Changed:
docs.text: • Bump to v1.2.5 with nore time to reaolve dworshak-prompt 0.2.18

docs.title: Changed:
docs.text: • Increase dworshak_prompt to 0.2.17 and dworshak_config to 0.2.1. ConfigManager - > DworshakConfig

docs.title: Fixed:
docs.text: • Add '--collect-submodules' flags for all libraries in the dworshak suite to build_executable.py

docs.title: Fixed:
docs.text: • uv sync with dworshak-prompt 0.2.15 and dworshak-secret 0.2.4, which celebrate the standard dworshak ecosystem extras, typer and crypto.

docs.title: Added:
docs.text: • dworshak-env

docs.title: Fixed:
docs.text: • Use pyhabitat.get_interp_shebang() with -p flag in shiv call in build_pyz.py.

docs.title: Changed:
docs.text: • To update the screenshots, we must first update typer-helptree to see recursively into nested sub apps.

docs.title: Fixed:
docs.text:
• Ensure build_executable.yml has uv installed
  • Guard against unmatched files during upload with continue on error true.

docs.title: Changed:
docs.text:
• Ensure build group carries shiv for pyz.
  • Zip onedir output.
  • suppress checksums

docs.title: Fixed:
docs.text:
• Correct exe chmoding for onedir and onefile
  • Generate checksums for all os matrix.
  • Ensure sanity check differentiation for windows.

docs.title: Fixed:
docs.text:
• Use uv build instead of python -m build in build_pyz.yml
  • Remove emojis from build_*.py to allow runner success on windows action.
  • chmod 0o755 in pyinstaller script for testing.

docs.title: Changed:
docs.text: • Let build_pyz.yml generate the tar.gz and .whl files for upload. Use wheel.unlink in build_pyz.py so that those are not uploaded but are destroyed.

docs.title: Added:
docs.text: • pyinstaller build script and runner

docs.title: Changed:
docs.text: • runner build.yml now triggers build_pyz.yml and build_executable.yml

docs.title: Internal:
docs.text: • Using build_executable.py is untenable on Termux because it cannot reasonably include cryptography from source (due to time to package)

docs.title: Internal:
docs.text: • Shiv is succeeding and crypto test is suceeding.

docs.title: Fixed:
docs.text: • Do not unlink the wheel in build_pyz.py, so that it can be checksummed and uploaded to release" [dev d067d38] shiv is succeeding and crypto test is suceeding. do not unlink the wheel, so that it can be checksummed and uploaded to release.

docs.title: Changed:
docs.text:
• build.yml includes extra optional dep group 'build', for shiv.
  • build_pyz.py uses shiv instead of zipapp.

docs.title: Internal:
docs.text: • dworshak-secret inspected for PYZ inclusiomln capacity, concerning cryptography eager import.

docs.title: Fixed:
docs.text: • build.yml

docs.title: Internal:
docs.text: • PYZ file needs sys and arch and version info in filename.

docs.title: BREAKING CHANGES:
docs.text:
• Flat app structure converted to nested app structure, with sub apps: secret, vault, config, and prompt.
  • Now dworshak exposes dworshak-secret and dworshak-prompt.
  • Soon dworshak will also expose dworshak-config.
  • dworshak-secret is the new name of the dworshak-acces package, for clarity

docs.title: Changed:
docs.text: • Update pyproject.toml description to not use bold markers (asterisk).

docs.title: Changed:
docs.text:
• Update screenshots in README
  • Update README installation guide and helptree call

docs.title: Changed:
docs.text:
• Add abbreviated flags for item and service name, -i and -s, in get and store commands.
  • Add "--value-only" flag to get command, for easier var assignment in the command line.
  • Add main file to enable python -m dworshak
  • Increase typer-helptree dependency to v0.1.10, with form add_typer_helptree(app, console, version = version, hidden=True)

docs.title: Internal:
docs.text: • Favor flags instad of positionals, for service and item values, for get and store commands. Explicit is better than implicit, and credential management does not need to be particularly fast. Short flags -s and -i help with speed.

docs.title: Changed:
docs.text:
• Bump dworshak-access to 1.1.1.
  • Magenta text in list command for Item column has been changed to green text, for visibility against PowerShell blue on a Windows 10 system.

docs.title: Changed:
docs.text: • Bump dworshak-access to 0.1.29.

docs.title: Internal:
docs.text: • Do not state the version number of dworshak-access in the optional dependency 'crypto' section. This is a maintenance risk to keep up with the number in two places.

docs.title: Changed:
docs.text:
• No longer does installation include crytography. User must explicity include it as an extra, [crypto].
  • Readme reflects new installation methods

docs.title: Fixed:
docs.text: • bumped dworshak-access version to 0.1.28, which now is 3.9 safe by using datetime.timezone.utc instead of datetime.UTC.

docs.title: Changed:
docs.text: • Claimed compatibility with Python 3.9

docs.title: Added:
docs.text:
• backup command
  • rotate-key command

docs.title: Changed:
docs.text: • dwroshak-access bumped to 0.1.23

docs.title: Added:
docs.text: • import and export functions

docs.title: Changed:
docs.text: • dwroshak-access bumped to 0.1.21

docs.title: Fixed:
docs.text: • Versioning is now handled by editing src/dworshak/VERSION

docs.title: Internal:
docs.text:
• Guard against edge cases like missing or malformed DB .
  • Version the DB schema so that i can be checked for compatibility.
  • Improve PYZ file naming to reflect the system, like pdflinkcheck, and not like pyhabitat.

docs.title: Added:
docs.text:
• build_pyz.py
  • pyhabitat as a dependency
  • typer-helptree as a dependency
  • .github/workflows/build.yml

docs.title: Removed
docs.text: • dev.py (which used to have the typer help tree)

docs.title: Added:
docs.text:
• help-tree
  • --version

docs.title: BREAKING:
docs.text:
• CLI commands changed for ergonomics and length:
    • retrieve -> get
    • register -> store

docs.title: Fixed:
docs.text: • username and password are out, secret is in

docs.title: Changed:
docs.text: • Now dworshak is just a CLI around dworshak-access.

docs.title: Added:
docs.text: • .github/workflows/ci.yml and .github/workflows/publish.yml. Now pushing to PyPI.

docs.title: Changed:
docs.text: • Remove uv-generated main.py file in root

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Initial project structure for dworshak.
  • cli.py with Typer support for setup, register, and list-services.
  • Secure SQLite vault logic using Fernet encryption.
  • Multi-platform support for .key storage in ~/.dworshak.

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text: • Credential storage risk by migrating from plaintext .env to encrypted SQL blobs.

docs.title: CLI Policy
docs.text:
Below is a clean, copy-paste–ready CLI policy document tailored to the Dworshak ecosystem and the code you’ve shown. It is written to be both developer-facing and test-enforcing.

You can put this in:

docs/cli-policy.md, or

CLI_POLICY.md at repo root, or

as a section in your main README.md

Dworshak CLI Policy

Version: 1.0 Applies to: dworshak, dworshak-secret, dworshak-config, dworshak-env, dworshak-prompt Audience: CLI developers, test authors, automation users

  1. Purpose

This document defines the behavioral contract for all Dworshak command-line interfaces.

The goals are:

Reliable bash scripting

Deterministic pytest CLI testing

Consistent human UX

Clear separation of data vs messaging

Predictable behavior across composed Typer apps

All Dworshak CLIs must follow this policy.

  1. Core Principle (Non-Negotiable)

STDOUT is for data. STDERR is for humans.

This rule applies universally and without exception.

  1. Output Channels

3.1 STDOUT (stdout)

STDOUT MUST contain only:

Machine-consumable values

Data intended for capture or piping

One value per command invocation (unless explicitly documented)

Examples:

Secret values

Env values

Prompt responses

JSON output (future)

STDOUT MUST NOT contain:

Status messages

Success messages

Rich output (tables, panels)

Explanations or context

3.2 STDERR (stderr)

STDERR MUST contain:

Human-readable messages

Status information

Warnings

Errors

Rich output (tables, panels, colors)

This includes:

rich.console.Console.print(...)

Confirmation prompts

Informational logging

  1. Exit Codes

Situation Exit Code

Successful operation 0 User cancelled / no-op 0 Expected failure (missing key, not found) 1 Invalid state (vault invalid, config corrupted) 1 Programmer error / unhandled exception non-zero

Exit codes are part of the API contract and must be tested.

  1. Global Flags (Required)

Every Dworshak CLI MUST implement the following flags at the root level:

5.1 --version

Prints version to STDOUT

Exits immediately with code 0

Produces no STDERR output

5.2 --quiet / -q

Purpose: Script-friendly mode

Behavior:

Suppresses all non-essential output

STDERR MUST be empty

STDOUT MAY contain data values

Rich output is disabled

Use cases:

Bash scripting

CI pipelines

Command substitution

Example:

PORT=$(dworshak env get PORT --quiet)

5.3 --verbose / -v

Purpose: Human-friendly mode

Behavior:

Enables detailed messaging

Rich output allowed

STDERR may contain explanations, context, progress

STDOUT behavior remains unchanged.

5.4 Flag precedence

If multiple flags are provided:

--quiet overrides --verbose

  1. Command Output Rules

6.1 Commands that return a value

Examples:

env get

secret get --value-only

prompt ask

obtain env

Rules:

Value → STDOUT

Context → STDERR

No trailing text on STDOUT

✅ Correct:

stdout: 8080 stderr: Stored [PORT] successfully.

❌ Incorrect:

stdout: Stored [PORT] successfully. 8080

6.2 Commands that mutate state

Examples:

secret set

env set

vault rotate-key

Rules:

Success/failure messages → STDERR

Optional resulting value → STDOUT (if meaningful)

No duplicate messaging

6.3 List / Table commands

Examples:

secret list

vault health

Rules:

Human display → STDERR

No STDOUT output unless explicitly documented

Rich tables MUST be sent to STDERR

  1. Rich Output Policy

All Rich output MUST go to STDERR.

Example:

console.print(table, stderr=True)

This ensures:

Bash pipelines remain clean

pytest stdout assertions are deterministic

  1. Prompting & Interactivity

8.1 Prompts

Prompts MUST NOT appear in --quiet mode

Prompts MUST respect non-interactive environments

CI detection must bypass prompts safely

8.2 Confirmation dialogs

Default choice MUST be safe ([y/N])

Cancellation exits with code 0

Cancellation MUST NOT emit STDOUT

  1. Error Handling

9.1 Expected errors

Examples:

Missing key

Secret not found

Config file missing

Rules:

Message → STDERR

Exit code → 1

No STDOUT output

9.2 Unexpected errors

Stack traces SHOULD be suppressed by default

Verbose mode MAY expose diagnostics

Exit code MUST be non-zero

  1. Testing Contract

Every CLI command MUST be testable with:

10.1 pytest + Typer runner

Tests must assert:

Exit code

STDOUT content

STDERR content

Example:

result = runner.invoke(app, ["env", "set", "PORT", "8080", "--quiet"])

assert result.exit_code == 0 assert result.stdout.strip() == "8080" assert result.stderr == ""

10.2 Subprocess (E2E)

Subprocess tests verify:

Entry point wiring

PATH resolution

Packaging behavior

They MUST NOT be the primary test mechanism.

  1. Composition Rules (dworshak root)

The dworshak CLI:

MUST NOT reimplement subcommand logic

MUST preserve output semantics of sub-CLIs

MAY add global flags and context

MUST forward arguments transparently

  1. Backward Compatibility

Breaking changes to:

STDOUT format

Exit codes

Flag behavior

REQUIRE a major version bump.

  1. Summary (Golden Rules)

  2. STDOUT = data

  3. STDERR = humans

  4. --quiet means only data

  5. Rich output never goes to STDOUT

  6. Exit codes are API

  7. Tests enforce the contract

  1. Compliance

All Dworshak CLI changes MUST:

Follow this policy

Include tests asserting stdout/stderr behavior

Avoid ad-hoc printing

docs.title: Dworshak CLI Policy
docs.text: Version: 1.0 Applies to: dworshak, dworshak-secret, dworshak-config, dworshak-env, dworshak-prompt Audience: CLI developers, test authors, automation users

docs.title: 1. Purpose
docs.text:
This document defines the behavioral contract for all Dworshak command-line interfaces.

The goals are:

  • Reliable bash scripting
  • Deterministic pytest CLI testing
  • Consistent human UX
  • Clear separation of data vs messaging
  • Predictable behavior across composed Typer apps

All Dworshak CLIs must follow this policy.

docs.title: 2. Core Principle (Non-Negotiable)
docs.text:
STDOUT is for data. STDERR is for humans.

This rule applies universally and without exception.

docs.title: 3.1 STDOUT (stdout)
docs.text:
STDOUT MUST contain only:

  • Machine-consumable values
  • Data intended for capture or piping
  • One value per command invocation (unless explicitly documented)

Examples:

  • Secret values
  • Env values
  • Prompt responses
  • JSON output (future)

STDOUT MUST NOT contain:

  • Status messages
  • Success messages
  • Rich output (tables, panels)
  • Explanations or context

docs.title: 3.2 STDERR (stderr)
docs.text:
STDERR MUST contain:

  • Human-readable messages
  • Status information
  • Warnings
  • Errors
  • Rich output (tables, panels, colors)

This includes:

  • rich.console.Console.print(...)
  • Confirmation prompts
  • Informational logging

docs.title: 4. Exit Codes
docs.text:
Situation Exit Code Successful operation 0 User cancelled / no-op 0 Expected failure (missing key, not found) 1 Invalid state (vault invalid, config corrupted) 1 Programmer error / unhandled exception non-zero

Exit codes are part of the API contract and must be tested.

docs.title: 5. Global Flags (Required)
docs.text: Every Dworshak CLI MUST implement the following flags at the root level:

docs.title: 5.1 --version
docs.text:
• Prints version to STDOUT
  • Exits immediately with code 0
  • Produces no STDERR output

docs.title: 5.2 --quiet / -q
docs.text:
Purpose: Script-friendly mode

Behavior:

  • Suppresses all non-essential output
  • STDERR MUST be empty
  • STDOUT MAY contain data values
  • Rich output is disabled

Use cases:

  • Bash scripting
  • CI pipelines
  • Command substitution

Example:

PORT=$(dworshak env get PORT --quiet)

docs.title: 5.3 --verbose / -v
docs.text:
Purpose: Human-friendly mode

Behavior:

  • Enables detailed messaging
  • Rich output allowed
  • STDERR may contain explanations, context, progress

STDOUT behavior remains unchanged.

docs.title: 5.4 Flag precedence
docs.text:
If multiple flags are provided:

--quiet overrides --verbose

docs.title: 6.1 Commands that return a value
docs.text:
Examples:

  • env get
  • secret get --value-only
  • prompt ask
  • obtain env

Rules:

  • Value → STDOUT
  • Context → STDERR
  • No trailing text on STDOUT

✅ Correct:

stdout: 8080
stderr: Stored [PORT] successfully.

❌ Incorrect:

stdout: Stored [PORT] successfully. 8080

docs.title: 6.2 Commands that mutate state
docs.text:
Examples:

  • secret set
  • env set
  • vault rotate-key

Rules:

  • Success/failure messages → STDERR
  • Optional resulting value → STDOUT (if meaningful)
  • No duplicate messaging

docs.title: 6.3 List / Table commands
docs.text:
Examples:

  • secret list
  • vault health

Rules:

  • Human display → STDERR
  • No STDOUT output unless explicitly documented
  • Rich tables MUST be sent to STDERR

docs.title: 7. Rich Output Policy
docs.text:
All Rich output MUST go to STDERR.

Example:

console.print(table, stderr=True)

This ensures:

  • Bash pipelines remain clean
  • pytest stdout assertions are deterministic

docs.title: 8.1 Prompts
docs.text:
• Prompts MUST NOT appear in --quiet mode
  • Prompts MUST respect non-interactive environments
  • CI detection must bypass prompts safely

docs.title: 8.2 Confirmation dialogs
docs.text:
• Default choice MUST be safe ([y/N])
  • Cancellation exits with code 0
  • Cancellation MUST NOT emit STDOUT

docs.title: 9.1 Expected errors
docs.text:
Examples:

  • Missing key
  • Secret not found
  • Config file missing

Rules:

  • Message → STDERR
  • Exit code → 1
  • No STDOUT output

docs.title: 9.2 Unexpected errors
docs.text:
• Stack traces SHOULD be suppressed by default
  • Verbose mode MAY expose diagnostics
  • Exit code MUST be non-zero

docs.title: 10. Testing Contract
docs.text: Every CLI command MUST be testable with:

docs.title: 10.1 pytest + Typer runner
docs.text:
Tests must assert:

  • Exit code
  • STDOUT content
  • STDERR content

Example:

result = runner.invoke(app, ["env", "set", "PORT", "8080", "--quiet"])

assert result.exit_code == 0
assert result.stdout.strip() == "8080"
assert result.stderr == ""

docs.title: 10.2 Subprocess (E2E)
docs.text:
Subprocess tests verify:

  • Entry point wiring
  • PATH resolution
  • Packaging behavior

They MUST NOT be the primary test mechanism.

docs.title: 11. Composition Rules (dworshak root)
docs.text:
The dworshak CLI:

  • MUST NOT reimplement subcommand logic
  • MUST preserve output semantics of sub-CLIs
  • MAY add global flags and context
  • MUST forward arguments transparently

docs.title: 12. Backward Compatibility
docs.text:
Breaking changes to:

  • STDOUT format
  • Exit codes
  • Flag behavior

REQUIRE a major version bump.

docs.title: 13. Summary (Golden Rules)
docs.text:
1. STDOUT = data
  2. STDERR = humans
  3. --quiet means only data
  4. Rich output never goes to STDOUT
  5. Exit codes are API
  6. Tests enforce the contract

docs.title: 14. Compliance
docs.text:
All Dworshak CLI changes MUST:

  • Follow this policy
  • Include tests asserting stdout/stderr behavior
  • Avoid ad-hoc printing

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: To ensure dworshak-prompt remains both human-friendly and automation-safe, all CLI commands must adhere to a strict Stream Separation Contract. This protocol defines how values, status messages, and diagnostics are routed to the user or the shell.

docs.title: 1. The Three Channels
docs.text:
We treat output as three distinct planes. A command's behavior is defined by which plane it is currently addressing.

Channel Purpose Accessibility stdout Data Plane: Raw values for machine consumption. Capturable by $(...) or pipes. stderr Human Plane: Feedback, status, and diagnostics. Always visible in terminal; does not pollute variables. tty Interactive Plane: Live user input (prompts). Managed by terminal device drivers (e.g., /dev/tty).

docs.title: --emit (The Data Gate)
docs.text:
• Purpose: Explicitly permits a value to be written to stdout.
  • Behavior: When present, the raw value is printed to stdout. When absent, stdout remains empty.
  • Security: This is the primary safety for secrets. No secret material should ever enter stdout without this flag.

docs.title: --verbose (The Diagnostic Gate)
docs.text:
• Purpose: Enables extra human-readable context.
  • Behavior: Prints detailed metadata (e.g., source of value, service name) to stderr.
  • Independence: Can be used with or without --emit. It never affects stdout.

docs.title: Value Emission (stdout)
docs.text:
If --emit is active:

  1. Print only the raw value.
  2. No labels (e.g., key: value).
  3. No Rich formatting or colors.
  4. No status messages.

docs.title: Status & Feedback (stderr)
docs.text:
Status messages should be provided by default to avoid "silent success," but they must stay off the data plane.

  • Basic Message (Default): A concise confirmation (e.g., Secret known.).
  • Hint (Default): If a value exists but --emit is missing, include: (use --emit to output value).
  • Verbose Message: Detailed info (e.g., Source: ~/.dworshak/vault.json).

docs.title: 4. Expected Behavior Matrix
docs.text: Command Intent stdout stderr get secret Simple Check (empty) Secret known. (use --emit to output value) get secret --emit Variable Capture my-password Secret known. get secret --verbose Inspect Source (empty) Secret known. Source: Vault. (use --emit to output value) get secret -ev Full Disclosure my-password Secret known. Source: Vault.

docs.title: 5. Implementation Rules for Developers
docs.text:
1. Never use print() for status messages. Use typer.echo(..., err=True) or a dedicated stderr logger.
  2. Never use Rich consoles for stdout if the output is intended to be a raw value.
  3. Exit Codes: Return 0 on success (value found/stored) and non-zero on failure or user cancellation.

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
pipx install "dworshak[crypto]"

dworshak setup

export USERNAME=$(dworshak prompt obtain secret fav_api username -e)

docs.title: config commands
docs.text:
[1m                                         [0m
[1m [0m[1;33mUsage: [0m[1mdworshak config [OPTIONS] [0m[1m      [0m[1m [0m
[1m [0m[1mCOMMAND [ARGS]...[0m[1m                      [0m[1m [0m
[1m                                         [0m

 configuration values to JSON. (v0.2.5)  

[2m╭─[0m[2m Options [0m[2m────────────────────────────[0m[2m─╮[0m
[2m│[0m [1;36m-[0m[1;36m-version[0m            Show the         [2m│[0m
[2m│[0m                      version.         [2m│[0m
[2m│[0m [1;36m-[0m[1;36m-help[0m     [1;32m-h[0m        Show this        [2m│[0m

docs.title: Obtain: Smart Retrieval with Interactive Fallback
docs.text: dworshak-prompt.Obtain is a unified mechanism to retrieve configuration, secrets, or environment values. Its purpose is to make workflows reliable, consistent, and CI-safe while reducing repetitive manual input.

docs.title: How Obtain Works
docs.text:
1. Check existing storage first

Secrets → dworshak-secret vault

Configurations → dworshak-config JSON files

Environment variables → .env files via dworshak-env

  1. Prompt only if needed

If the requested value is missing or overwrite=True, Obtain will ask the user for input.

Input is collected using a priority-aware interface sequence:

  1. Console (terminal)

  2. GUI (Tkinter)

  3. Web (local browser server)

Incompatible modes are automatically skipped. CI environments or non-interactive shells can return defaults immediately.

  1. Automatic persistence

Unless forget=True, any new or updated value is saved back to the original storage (vault, config, or env file).

docs.title: Data Structures
docs.text:
Class Purpose

ObtainResult Generic wrapper for a retrieved value SecretData Overrides status_message for secrets ConfigData Alias for ObtainResult for config values EnvData Alias for ObtainResult for environment variables

ObtainResult contains:

  • value: Optional[str] — the retrieved value

  • is_new: Optional[bool] — True if stored just now, False if already known, None if user cancelled

  • status_message: str — human-readable status

docs.title: Using Obtain in Python
docs.text:
from dworshak_prompt import Obtain

# Initialize the manager
obtain = Obtain()

# Retrieve a secret (check vault first, prompt if missing)
secret_val = obtain.secret("my_service", "api_key")

# Retrieve a config value (check file first, prompt if missing)
config_val = obtain.config("my_service", "timeout", suggestion="30")

# Retrieve an environment value (check .env first, prompt if missing)
env_val = obtain.env("DATABASE_URL")

docs.title: One-Liner Functional API
docs.text:
dworshak_obtain provides a concise functional interface:

from dworshak_prompt.obtain import dworshak_obtain, StoreMode

# Secret
api_key = dworshak_obtain("my_service", "api_key", store=StoreMode.SECRET)

# Config
timeout = dworshak_obtain("my_service", "timeout", store=StoreMode.CONFIG)

# Environment
db_url = dworshak_obtain("DATABASE_URL", store=StoreMode.ENV)

This automatically selects the correct backend and triggers the interactive prompt only when necessary.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Storage-aware first: never prompts unnecessarily

  • Fallback interfaces: console → GUI → web

  • CI-safe: non-interactive environments return defaults

  • Persistent by default: stores new values automatically

Flexible: supports secrets, config files, and environment variables

docs.title: Vault Architecture
docs.text: The Dworshak Vault is the central storage system for all secrets, configurations, and environment data. It is designed with security, auditability, and ease-of-use in mind, allowing users and automated processes to safely retrieve and store sensitive information. The default path for the vault is at ~/.dworshak/vault.md, and is paired with a .key file at the same location. Vault management via CLI is provided by the dworshak secret vault sub app, and is defined in the dworshak-secret package.

docs.title: Dworshak Ecosystem Overview
docs.text:
The Dworshak ecosystem is designed to provide secure, reliable, and automated configuration and credential management for applications and CI/CD pipelines. It consists of several complementary tools:

  • dworshak-secret: Encrypted secret storage and retrieval.
  • dworshak-config: Persistent, plaintext configuration storage.
  • dworshak-env: Environment variable management with .env integration.
  • dworshak-prompt: Multiplexed user input and prompting engine.
  • dworshak CLI: Central orchestrator for interacting with all tools.

Each tool can operate independently, but together they form a robust workflow for managing credentials, configuration, and environment values in a user-friendly and secure manner.

docs.title: dworshak-secret
docs.text: dworshak-secret is the secure vault component of the Dworshak ecosystem.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• AES-256 encrypted storage
  • Per-service secret management
  • Secret rotation and key management
  • CI/CD friendly

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
dworshak secret vault setup
dworshak prompt obtain secret maxson-eds api_key
